title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false
input_row.rationale: Profiling allows for the identification of specific performance bottlenecks. It generates a significant amount of program data that could potentially be exploited to uncover system and program details. If you are not experiencing any bottlenecks and do not need the profiler for troubleshooting purposes, it is recommended to turn it off to reduce the potential attack surface.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-controller-manager Verify that the --profiling argument is set to false.
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and set "--profiling=false": kubectl edit deployments federation-controller-manager-deployment -- namespace=federation-system Impact: Profiling information would not be available. Default Value: By default, profiling is enabled.   223 | P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/federation-controller-manager/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/profi ling.md 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/federation/manifests/fe deration-controller-manager-deployment.yaml
input_row.impact: Profiling information would not be available. Default Value: By default, profiling is enabled.   223 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_federation_controller_manager_profiling_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubernetes API for the Deployment named federation-controller-manager-deployment in the federation-system namespace.
- Inspect the Pod template spec for all containers within this Deployment.
- For each container, examine the command and args fields to verify the presence and value of the --profiling argument.
- Pass Criteria: All federation-controller-manager containers must explicitly set --profiling=false.
- Fail Criteria: Any federation-controller-manager container is missing the --profiling argument or has it set to true (or not set, as the default is true).
- Example kubectl command:
  ```
  kubectl -n federation-system get deployment federation-controller-manager-deployment -o json | jq '.spec.template.spec.containers[].args'
  ```
- Alternatively, use the Kubernetes API to fetch the Deployment spec and parse the args.
- Confirm compliance by ensuring every instance of --profiling is set to false for all federation-controller-manager containers.
